free_text: Constraining the destruction rate of 40K in stellar
nucleosynthesis through the study of the 40Ar(p,n)40K
tandem accelerator of the Edwards Accelerator
Natural argon gas of 99.999% purity in a gas cell
with 3 cm in length and 9 mm internal diameter. The
entrance window was made of high purity (99.5%) Al
with thickness of 10.37 mu-m. The gas pressure was
198 torr, which corresponds to an areal density of
Neutrons were detected using BC-408 plastic
scintillator bar with dimensions of 310 x 45 x 25 mm3
Detector was palced in the time-of-flight tunnel at a
distance of 5 m from the terget. Swinger magnet could
be placed at lab. angles between 0 and 180deg. Angular
resolution was 0.25 deg in lab and time resolution
Gamma rays were detected using LaBr3(Ce)
scintillator with dimensions 4.5 x 5.0 cm. Detector
was mounted at an angle 90deg relative to beam axis
at a distance of 18 cm from the target.
Neutron energy was determined by time of flight.
